TSLA: Top Picks 2026: Kodiak AI Inc. (KDK). Eoin Treacy highlights Kodiak AI Inc. (KDK) as a top pick for 2026, anticipating a breakthrough year for autonomous vehicles due to upcoming DOT and NHTSA regulatory changes. While Tesla and Waymo compete in the autonomous driving space, Kodiak AI offers modular sensor pods for commercial vehicles, providing an indirect play on the trend without the need for large data centers. The company's technology, which converts conventional trucks into autonomous rigs, is seen as leading the disruption in the haulage sector.

Tesla (TSLA) to increase the price of Cybertruck Standard, extends delivery date due to increased demand. Tesla is set to increase the price of its Cybertruck Standard AWD variant after February 28, following a short period at the initial $59,990 price. The significant demand for this new, more affordable trim has also led to extended delivery dates, now estimated for September-October 2026, up from June. This strategy has been viewed by some customers and influencers as a "market gimmick."

Tesla must face lawsuit alleging anti-American bias in hiring, US judge rules. A U.S. judge has ruled that Tesla must face a lawsuit alleging anti-American bias in its hiring practices, allowing a software engineer's proposed class action to proceed. The lawsuit claims Tesla discriminates against American citizens to hire foreign workers at lower pay, particularly H-1B visa holders. While allowing the case to move forward based on a recruiter's comment, the judge expressed skepticism about the strength of the overall evidence presented so far.

EV Sales Falling, But EV Owners Remain Loyal, JD Power Says. Despite a predicted decline in EV sales following the end of federal tax credits, a new JD Power study indicates surging owner satisfaction. The 2026 U.S. Electric Vehicle Experience Ownership Study found that 96% of EV owners plan to purchase another EV, citing improvements in battery technology, charging infrastructure, and vehicle performance. Satisfaction with charging station reliability and vehicle quality (e.g., reduced squeaks/rattles) has notably increased, with the Tesla Model 3, Tesla Model Y, and BMW i4 topping premium segments, and the Ford Mustang Mach-E leading mass-market vehicles.
TGT: Protein Pop Launches Protein Pop Plus at Costco Nationwide. Rise Wellness has launched "Protein Pop Plus" at all 607 Costco locations across the United States. This new carbonated, clear protein beverage offers 30 grams of protein, a blend of whey protein isolate and bovine collagen, zero sugar, and no artificial sweeteners or caffeine. The expansion follows the successful debut of the original Protein Pop at Natural Products Expo West and its subsequent launch in Target stores, solidifying the brand's position in the functional beverage market.

Citi Raises Costco (COST) Price Target to $1,000 from $990. Citi analyst Steven Zaccone raised Costco's price target to $1,000 from $990, maintaining a Neutral rating, following a modest beat in fiscal second-quarter sales and earnings. Similarly, Evercore ISI also raised its price target for Costco to $1,050. The company reported strong Q1 fiscal 2026 results with an 8.2% rise in net sales and plans for continued global warehouse expansion.

Smart eyewear pioneer Lucyd expands premium brand portfolio with Reebok, Eddie Bauer, and Nautica Collections. Innovative Eyewear, operating under the Lucyd brand, is expanding its smart eyewear portfolio by partnering with established consumer brands like Reebok, Eddie Bauer, and Nautica. This strategy aims to accelerate adoption by integrating smart eyewear technology into recognized brands, targeting diverse customer segments, and leveraging existing retail channels. The company's Lucyd app, featuring the first voice interface for ChatGPT, serves as a unified platform across all its products, indicating a move towards broad utility and device-agnostic AI accessibility.
SBUX: Why Starbucks (SBUX) Stock Is Down Today. Starbucks (SBUX) stock fell 3.4% following the Trump administration's announcement of new global tariffs, reigniting trade policy uncertainty. This move, utilizing the Trade Act of 1974 after a Supreme Court ruling limited other options, created concerns about the impact on international supply chains and corporate earnings. While Starbucks shares are up 13.1% year-to-date, they remain 18% below their 52-week high, and past tariff announcements have also led to significant drops.

Starbucks Corp. stock outperforms competitors on strong trading day. Starbucks Corp. (SBUX) stock increased by 1.83% to $96.51, outperforming its competitors on a strong trading day where both the S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average also saw gains. The stock closed 17.84% below its 52-week high.
DIS: Disney Communications Chief Kristina Schake to Exit the Company Next Month. Kristina Schake, Disney's Senior Executive VP and Chief Communications Officer, is set to leave the company at the end of March, coinciding with CEO Bob Iger's departure. Josh D'Amaro will succeed Iger as CEO, and Dana Walden will become president and chief creative officer. Both Iger and Schake sent memos to staff, thanking Schake for her significant contributions during a pivotal period for Disney.

Chief Communications Officer Kristina Schake to Leave Walt Disney Company Next Month. Kristina Schake, Chief Communications Officer for The Walt Disney Company, is set to depart next month on March 18, 2026, the same day Josh D'Amaro assumes the CEO position. Schake joined Disney in June 2022 under Bob Chapek and, after initially earning an extended contract through 2027, will now leave as part of an anticipated C-suite restructuring. Her departure follows a significant career in politics and will precede further executive announcements under D'Amaro's leadership.

Kristina Schake To Depart As Chief Communications Officer Of The Walt Disney Company. Kristina Schake, Senior Executive Vice President and Chief Communications Officer of The Walt Disney Company, will depart the company after March 18, 2026, coinciding with the end of Bob Iger’s tenure as CEO. Schake, who joined in 2022, played a crucial role in strengthening Disney's communications strategies during a period of significant change and strategic initiatives. Her successor will be announced at a later date.

Gap Inc. Remakes Its Loyalty Program. Gap Inc. has launched "Encore," a new loyalty program designed to deepen engagement with its brands (Gap, Old Navy, Banana Republic, and Athleta) and connect consumers with pop culture. The program offers tiered benefits, early access to exclusive products, and unique experiences in fashion and entertainment through partnerships with companies like Disney, NBCUniversal, Live Nation, and AMC Theatres. Existing loyalty members will automatically transition to Encore, which also includes an optional Mastercard for enhanced rewards.

Walt Disney Co. stock underperforms Tuesday when compared to competitors despite daily gains. Shares of Walt Disney Co. rose 1.57% on Tuesday to $106.05, breaking a three-day losing streak, despite the stock underperforming compared to competitors. This occurred during an overall favorable trading session where the S&P 500 Index and Dow Jones Industrial Average also saw gains.

The Walt Disney Company Announces Departure of Kristina Schake as Senior Executive Vice President and Chief Communications Officer, Effective After March 18, 2026. Kristina Schake, Senior Executive Vice President and Chief Communications Officer for The Walt Disney Company, will depart after March 18, 2026, coinciding with the end of Bob Iger's CEO tenure. Schake, who joined in 2022, played a crucial role in the company's strategic communications, including navigating proxy challenges, achieving streaming profitability, and announcing new leadership. Her extensive background includes roles in government and tech, such as leading the nationwide COVID-19 vaccine campaign and serving as Communications Director for First Lady Michelle Obama.
